title: AGENTS.md
text: erplots provides fluent mini-language building exposure-response plots: model curves/ribbons, quantile-binned response-rate/mean summaries, raw-data layer, grouped distribution panels. model-agnostic: erplots never fits model . model implementing er_predict() can visualised; implementing er_simulate() er_summary() additionally enables uncertainty spaghetti plots/VPCs summary annotations (e.g. p-values). See ?er_model_interface. er_plot() takes response_type = c("auto", "binary", "continuous", "count") argument (auto-detected response column given: logical values entirely {0, 1} → "binary", else "continuous"; "auto" never resolves "count" – must declared explicitly), stored object$response$type/ object$response$limits. model curve/ribbon (er_plot_show_model()) group panel (er_plot_show_groups()) layers work response type dispatch needed. quantile summary layer (er_plot_show_quantiles()) er_vpc_plot() now fully generalised across three response types (rate + Clopper-Pearson "binary"; mean + t-interval "continuous"; mean + exact Poisson interval "count"). data layer (er_plot_show_data(), renamed er_plot_show_datastrip()) one remaining binary-layer – still errors (rather silently mis-plot) "continuous"/"count" responses, continuous-response variant (single color-encoded panel rather current two-panel jitter strip) scoped yet built – see “Planned work” . companion package erglm (formerly erlr) fits GLM-based exposure-response models implements package’s generics model objects. erplots hard dependency erglm – ’s listed Suggests, examples/tests/vignettes. Since erglm GitHub-(CRAN), DESCRIPTION also declares Remotes: djnavarro/erglm CI can resolve . Key API points remember: example dataset erglm::erglm_data (erlr::lr_data), models fit erglm::erglm_model(formula, data, family = ...) (family explicit). erglm now genuinely supports binomial/poisson/gaussian/Gamma families, matching binary (ae1/ae2), count (ae_count), continuous (biomarker_change, ae_duration) response columns erglm_data – just binomial. Models simulated erglm::erglm_vpc_sim(); er_predict()/er_simulate()/ er_summary() methods erglm registers model objects correct four families, erplots-side changes needed .

title: AGENTS.md
text: R/er-generics.R – model interface: er_predict(), er_simulate(), er_summary() generics default methods. R/er-plot-api.R – public plot-building API: er_plot(), er_plot_show_model(), er_plot_show_quantiles(), er_plot_show_data(), er_plot_show_groups(), er_plot_build(), plus print/plot methods er_plot S3 class. layer function (except er_plot() ) dedicated Rd topic (shared @rdname). R/er-plot-part.R – internal .part_*() functions assemble configuration plot component (er_predict() / er_simulate() / er_summary() get called user-supplied model). R/er-plot-build.R, R/er-plot-compose.R – internal plotting/layout machinery turns parts ggplot2 objects composes patchwork. R/er-plot-partials-*.R – pluggable build_*() partial builders (one file per component: model, summary, quantile, data, group). See ?er_partial interface builders share. R/er-vpc.R – er_vpc_plot(), model-agnostic VPC-style plot operating plain observed/simulated data frames. R/utils-helpers.R, R/utils-global.R – small internal helpers (including binary-response-clopper_pearson(), cut_quantile(), cut_exposure_quantile(), response-type detector .detect_response_type(), shared guard-rail helper .abort_continuous_unsupported()) globalVariables() declarations NSE.

title: erplots development plan
text: erplots designed model-agnostic fitted model side (model implementing er_predict()/er_simulate()/er_summary() can plotted), several data layers still hardcode assumption observed response binary (0/1). Generalising layers let erplots visualise, e.g., continuous-response exposure-response models (dose-response/emax-style models) well logistic-regression style binary-response models, without changing model-side contract. longer hypothetical. erglm (reference model package) generalised binomial-(erlr) erglm_model(formula, data, family = ...) supporting binomial/poisson/gaussian/Gamma, example data (erglm::erglm_data) now ships genuine continuous (biomarker_change) count (ae_count, ae_duration) response columns alongside original binary ones (ae1/ae2). er_predict()/er_simulate()/er_summary() contract honoured correctly families – part integration needs work. fitting erglm_model(biomarker_change ~ aucss, erglm_data, family = gaussian()) passing er_plot_show_quantiles() confirms failure mode predicted : error, silently renders nonsense (error bars pinned y = 0/1, computed sum(response == 1) / sum(response == 0) response never exactly 0 1). er_vpc_plot() identical bug. Since users can now trivially construct exactly kind model, generalising layers moved “nice ” main blocker saying erplots supports “erglm model”.

title: erplots development plan
text: Quantile summary layer (er_plot_show_quantiles(), .part_quantile(), build_quantile_errorbar()): computes, per exposure bin, n1/n0 counts Clopper-Pearson CI response rate. continuous response instead compute mean CI mean (e.g. t-based, SE-based). Needs response-type-dispatched summary step – likely small internal generic (analogous existing build_* partial pattern) summarise_quantile.binary() / summarise_quantile.continuous() implementations, selected either explicit argument auto-detecting whether response column binary (values {0, 1}, logical). Data strip layer (er_plot_show_datastrip(), build_datastrip_jitter()): “responders line, non-responders ” two-panel jitter strip inherently binary-response visualisation. doesn’t obvious continuous analogue (plain exposure-vs-response scatter closest equivalent, ’s different plot, variant one). layer likely need genuinely different design continuous responses, simply offered continuous-response plots. er_vpc_plot(): currently compares observed vs. simulated response rates (Clopper-Pearson CIs observed side, simulation quantiles simulated side). Generalises fairly directly comparing means (appropriate CI observed mean) – easiest three generalise. Response-type detection/declaration: needs decision whether response type auto-detected (e.g., “binary” response column takes values {0, 1}/logical, “continuous” otherwise) explicitly declared (e.g. response_type argument er_plot()). Auto-detection convenient failure modes (e.g. 0/1 count variable isn’t actually binary response); explicit argument sensible auto-detected default probably safer choice. Count (Poisson-like) responses: clearly binary continuous. Treating “continuous” (mean + CI) probably adequate approximation first pass, worth flagging known simplification rather deciding definitively now. er_plot()’s hardcoded response scale: er_plot() sets object$response$limits <- c(0, 1) unconditionally (R/er-plot-part.R), .part_model()’s p-value-label placement (config$corner_distance) computes distance four plot corners assuming y/fit_resp already lives [0, 1] (never rescales object$response$limits data’s range). continuous response silently places (fails place) p-value label using wrong scale. mentioned original version plan – found confirming quantile-layer bug . Needs part response-type work even though sits “already generalises free” model/ribbon code path, quantile/strip/VPC layers.

text: questions originally left open; now working recommendation implementation isn’t blocked, still debate new information changes calculus. Response-type detection. Add explicit response_type argument (probably er_plot(), since governs builders valid whole plot, just one component), defaulting "auto". auto-detection heuristic: values entirely {0, 1} logical column → "binary", otherwise → "continuous". mirrors “automatic escape hatch” pattern already adopted erlr side (e.g. SCM test-selection decision) – convenient default, overridable heuristic gets wrong (e.g. genuine 0/1 count variable). Data strip continuous responses. [Superseded – see “Continuous- response data strip” section .] Originally: omit v1, documented limitation, rather design continuous-specific replacement now. two-panel “responders line, non-responders ” design structurally binary flag – ’s variant geometry continuous response, different plot entirely (e.g. rug raw scatter), building speculatively anyone needs isn’t worth design effort yet. Revisit /concrete use case shows . revisit now happened – see scoped design. Quantile-bin CI method continuous responses. Use t-interval, bootstrap. mirrors convention erlr/erglm’s generalisation plan settled analogous problem (families estimated dispersion – gaussian, Gamma – use Pr(>|t|)-based inference, chi-squared/asymptotic-normal approximation), two packages stay consistent, ’s simpler implement explain bootstrap. Count responses. [Fast-follow implemented.] v1 (Stage 4) treated “continuous” (mean ± CI via t-interval (3)), documented known approximation, remains default behaviour response_type = "auto" (count response still auto-detects "continuous", since .detect_response_type() deliberately left unchanged – see ). planned fast-follow – exact Poisson CI path, analogous binary path uses exact Clopper-Pearson interval rather normal approximation – now added fourth, explicitly opt-response_type = "count" value er_plot()/er_vpc_plot(). swaps t-interval approximation [poisson_interval()] (bin mean ± exact Poisson/Garwood interval via qgamma()), – unlike t-interval – never produces negative lower bound low-count bin. "auto" intentionally never resolves "count" ("binary"/"continuous"), purely additive: existing auto-detected explicitly-"continuous" count-response code unaffected, callers opt exact interval naming response_type = "count" . Files: R/utils-helpers.R (poisson_interval(), .abort_continuous_unsupported() gaining response_type parameter message names actual offending type), R/er-plot-part.R (.part_quantile()’s new "count" branch), R/er-vpc.R (er_vpc_plot()’s new "count" branch smm_obs; smm_sim needed changes, expected), R/er-plot-api.R (er_plot()’s match.arg() choices er_plot_show_datastrip()’s guard, now also fires – accurate “support count responses” message – declared "count" response, just "continuous"), R/utils-global.R (n_units global). Docs: ?er_plot, ?er_vpc_plot, vignettes/articles/plot.Rmd updated describe demonstrate new value. Tests: poisson_interval() unit tests test-utils-helpers.R (matches stats::poisson.test(), brackets rate, respects conf_level, sums vector counts, never negative zero counts); response_type = "count" round-trips added test-er-plot-part.R test-er-vpc.R (asserting ci_lower >= 0, unlike t-interval path); new er_plot_show_datastrip() error-message test declared count response test-er-plot-api.R. Done : erglm_model(ae_count ~ aucss, erglm_data, family = poisson()) round-trips er_plot_show_quantiles() er_vpc_plot() response_type = "count" producing non-negative exact Poisson interval, without disturbing existing "auto"/"continuous" count-response behaviour response type. Met – devtools::check() clean (0/0/0), 270 tests passing (248). Coordination erglm. Superseded – erglm’s continuous/count response support landed (gaussian/Gamma/poisson families, biomarker_change/ae_count/ae_duration columns erglm_data). implementation roadmap uses erglm test models directly rather stubbing local glm()/lm() fixture, since real dependency now available exercising directly (rather hand-rolled stand-) better test actual integration.

what: Layer composition semantics: singleton vs. additive
title: erplots development plan
text: Documenting current (undocumented) behavior: er_plot_show_model(), er_plot_show_quantiles(), er_plot_show_data() (renamed er_plot_show_datastrip() – see “Naming decision” ) singleton – calling one twice overwrites object$part$<x>, silently discarding first call’s result. er_plot_show_groups() additive – call’s group_by accumulates named entry, multiple calls produce multiple side--side group panels rather one call replacing another. Proposed reading: intentional, accidental, reflects reasonable mental model – one “model” one “quantile summary” per plot, many legitimate ways slice exposure distribution different grouping variables. Recommend documenting distinction explicitly (?er_plot’s @details, ?er_partial, new vignette material ) rather changing behavior. flagged open question rather assumed, since ’s real design commitment worth confirming rather docs-formality. One related loose end, resolved future-rather left open: er_plot_show_model() singleton, overlaying two model curves (e.g. comparing candidate model null/reference model, Emax fit linear one) isn’t possible today. three singleton layers, one genuine plausible use case – unlike quantile data, calling layer twice response variable obvious distinct meaning (overlaid bin-counts panel subsets just visually ambiguous, informative). Recommendation: don’t change anything now – concrete request exists yet, making model additive real work, comparable Stage 7 already make data layer’s strata facets legend-aware (model layer need analogous per-model legend/label story). Mirrors design decision (2)’s original stance data strip: leave door open, revisit /concrete need shows . Quantile data layers expected ever need – model flagged plausible future additive layer.

what: Building a plot is composing layers onto an object
title: The plotting grammar
text: er_plot() creates empty object class er_plot, storing data plot’s exposure/response/stratification variables. pipe one layer functions, adds one visual component, finish plot()/print() ([er_plot_build()] directly):  currently four layers, documented help topic:
code: mod <- erglm_model(ae1 ~ aucss, erglm_data, family = binomial())  erglm_data |>   er_plot(aucss, ae1) |>   er_plot_show_model(mod) |>   er_plot_show_quantiles() |>   er_plot_show_groups(aucss) |>   plot() observed data       |       v   er_plot()                       -- creates the (empty) object       |       v   layer functions (piped, any order, any subset):     er_plot_show_model()     er_plot_show_quantiles()     er_plot_show_data()     er_plot_show_groups()       |       v   er_plot_build()                 -- called for you by plot()/print()       |       v   polish: margins, labels, legends, theme       |       v   compose: patchwork       |       v   rendered plot

what: Layers are either singleton or additive
title: The plotting grammar
text: Calling layer function twice object doesn’t always thing. model, quantile, data layers singleton: second call replaces first call’s result rather combining two. group layer one exception: ’s additive. call adds another panel alongside already added, rather replacing : deliberate design choice, oversight: one “model” one “quantile summary” show per plot, many legitimate ways slice exposure distribution different grouping variables. one flagged exception watch : model layer singleton, overlaying two model curves comparison (e.g. candidate model reference model) isn’t currently possible. ’s tracked plausible future addition, planned work – see PLAN.md.
code: plt <- erglm_data |>   er_plot(aucss, ae1) |>   er_plot_show_quantiles(bins = 4) |>   er_plot_show_quantiles(bins = 8) # overwrites the bins = 4 call  plt$part$quantile$config$n_quantiles # 8, not 4 #> [1] 8 plt <- erglm_data |>   er_plot(aucss, ae1) |>   er_plot_show_groups(aucss) |>   er_plot_show_groups(treatment) # adds a second panel, doesn't replace the first  names(plt$part$group$config) # both grouping variables are still there #> [1] "treatment"

what: Stratification composes with layers, usually via color
title: The plotting grammar
text: stratify_by, set er_plot(), declares single discrete variable used split layers color/fill, one shared, deduplicated legend across whole composed plot:  layer’s keep_strata argument controls whether layer uses stratification (default TRUE whenever stratify_by set). general rule, order layer actually applies : layer’s encoding takes precedence; stratification adapts whatever channel left, defaulting color/fill. Today, every layer’s encoding leaves color/fill free strata, rule invisible practice – stops invisible layer needs color something else. data layer first anticipated case: continuous-response variant need color response value , stratification one layer fall back per-stratum facets instead. variant doesn’t exist yet (see PLAN.md’s “Continuous-response data strip” section); today’s data layer supports binary response, uses color/fill strata like every layer.
code: mod_strat <- erglm_model(ae1 ~ aucss + sex, erglm_data, family = binomial())  erglm_data |>   er_plot(aucss, ae1, stratify_by = sex) |>   er_plot_show_model(mod_strat) |>   er_plot_show_quantiles() |>   er_plot_show_data() |>   plot()

what: Response type changes what a layer summarises, not whether it appears
title: The plotting grammar
text: response_type, also set er_plot() ("auto", "binary", "continuous", "count"), governs response’s scale summary/CI method response-type-aware layer uses. model group layers don’t look response’s type – consume [er_predict()]/[er_simulate()] output exposure variable, respectively. quantile layer dispatches directly: "auto" classifies response "binary" ’s logical confined {0, 1}, "continuous" otherwise – genuine count response auto-detects "continuous" summarised approximately-continuous quantity unless response_type = "count" declared explicitly. See [er_plot_show_quantiles()] full rationale Plotting article’s “Continuous responses” section worked examples, including case choice t-interval exact Poisson interval visibly matters. data layer odd one : rather adapting summary statistic, currently supports "binary" responses , errors "continuous"/"count" rather silently mis-plotting – see [er_plot_show_data()].

Treat design change isn’t reflected incomplete.

title: Plotting
text: Stratification adds colour across components. requires model includes stratification variable term:  can suppress stratification specific components
code: mod_strat <- erglm_model(ae1 ~ aucss + sex, erglm_data, family = binomial())  erglm_data |>    er_plot(     exposure = aucss,      response = ae1,      stratify_by = sex   ) |>    er_plot_show_model(mod_strat) |>    er_plot_show_quantiles() |>    er_plot_show_data() |>   plot() erglm_data |>    er_plot(     exposure = aucss,      response = ae1,      stratify_by = sex   ) |>    # keep_strata = FALSE needs a model that doesn't include the   # stratification variable, so we pass the un-stratified `mod` here   er_plot_show_model(mod, keep_strata = FALSE) |>    er_plot_show_quantiles() |>    er_plot_show_data() |>   plot()

title: Plotting
text: er_plot() auto-detects whether response binary (logical, values entirely {0, 1}) continuous, can override detection response_type. model component quantile component adapt: quantile bins report mean t-interval instead rate Clopper-Pearson interval. data layer component continuous-response variant (see ), ’s omitted .  er_vpc_plot() generalises way, comparing observed vs. simulated means rather rates:  Count (Poisson-style) responses, adverse-event count, auto-detected "continuous" summarised way (bin mean plus t-interval) unless declare response_type = "count" explicitly:  Declaring response_type = "count" swaps t-interval approximation exact Poisson interval (bin mean plus [poisson_interval()] instead [t_interval()]), never produces negative lower bound – useful low-count bins, t-interval approximation can. erglm_data’s ae_count, none bin means low enough actually happen, two plots look almost identical re-ran last one response_type = "count". make difference concrete, ’s synthetic dataset placebo arm 2 events among 20 subjects: default (t-interval) path’s placebo-arm error bar dips visibly zero – nonsensical negative event rate:   Declaring response_type = "count" keeps placebo-arm point estimate replaces t-interval exact Poisson interval, stays non-negative:   declaration works er_vpc_plot():
code: mod_gaussian <- erglm_model(biomarker_change ~ aucss, erglm_data, family = gaussian())  erglm_data |>    er_plot(aucss, biomarker_change) |>    er_plot_show_model(mod_gaussian) |>    er_plot_show_quantiles() |>    plot() sim_gaussian <- erglm_vpc_sim(mod_gaussian, seed = 3947) er_vpc_plot(erglm_data, sim_gaussian, aucss, biomarker_change, group_by = aucss) mod_poisson <- erglm_model(ae_count ~ aucss, erglm_data, family = poisson())  erglm_data |>    er_plot(aucss, ae_count) |>    er_plot_show_model(mod_poisson) |>    er_plot_show_quantiles() |>    plot() set.seed(84) placebo_counts <- rpois(20, 0.05) while (sum(placebo_counts) != 2) placebo_counts <- rpois(20, 0.05)  aucss_dosed <- sort(runif(80, 100, 3000)) count_dosed <- rpois(80, 0.1 + 0.003 * aucss_dosed)  low_count_data <- data.frame(   aucss = c(rep(0, 20), aucss_dosed),   ae_count = c(placebo_counts, count_dosed) )  mod_low_count <- erglm_model(ae_count ~ aucss, low_count_data, family = poisson()) low_count_data |>    er_plot(aucss, ae_count) |>    er_plot_show_model(mod_low_count) |>    er_plot_show_quantiles() |>    plot() +   ggplot2::coord_cartesian(ylim = c(-0.5, 1.5), xlim = c(-50, 800)) #> Coordinate system already present. #> ℹ Adding new coordinate system, which will replace the existing one. low_count_data |>    er_plot(aucss, ae_count, response_type = "count") |>    er_plot_show_model(mod_low_count) |>    er_plot_show_quantiles() |>    plot() +   ggplot2::coord_cartesian(ylim = c(-0.5, 1.5), xlim = c(-50, 800)) #> Coordinate system already present. #> ℹ Adding new coordinate system, which will replace the existing one. sim_poisson <- erglm_vpc_sim(mod_poisson, seed = 6142) er_vpc_plot(   erglm_data, sim_poisson, aucss, ae_count, group_by = aucss,   response_type = "count" )

title: Model-Agnostic Exposure-Response Plots
text: erplots provides fluent mini-language building exposure-response plots: model curves/ribbons, quantile-binned response-rate summaries, data strips, grouped distribution panels. model-agnostic: erplots never fits model . Instead, fit model whatever package suits workflow (e.g. erglm logistic regression), pass fitted object er_plot_show_model(). model implements er_predict() can visualised; implementing er_simulate() er_summary() additionally enables uncertainty spaghetti plots/VPCs summary annotations (e.g. p-values). See ?er_model_interface.

what: Model interface for exposure-response plots — er_model_interface
title: Model interface for exposure-response plots — er_model_interface
text: erplots draws exposure-response plots fitted model implements small interface, rather assuming particular model class (e.g. logistic regression glm). make model usable er_plot_show_model() friends, implement least method er_predict(). Implementing er_simulate() additionally enables simulation-based visualisations (e.g. spaghetti plots, VPCs); implementing er_summary() enables annotations p-value labels.

title: Model interface for exposure-response plots — er_model_interface

title: Model interface for exposure-response plots — er_model_interface
text: model fitted exposure-response model object newdata data frame covariate values predict conf_level Confidence level prediction interval ... Passed methods nsim Number simulation replicates seed Optional RNG seed

title: Model interface for exposure-response plots — er_model_interface
text: er_predict() returns newdata three additional columns: fit_resp (point prediction), ci_lower, ci_upper. er_simulate() returns data frame containing nsim replicates newdata, sim_id column identifying replicate, fit_resp column giving simulated prediction replicate (reflecting parameter uncertainty). Models support simulation-based visualisation implement method; default method returns NULL; callers treat NULL result "available" rather error. er_summary() returns named list scalar summary statistics (example list(p_value = 0.013)), NULL nothing available. default method returns NULL.

title: The exposure-response plotting mini-language — er_plot
text: er_plot() creates (empty) plot object S3 class er_plot. Build plot piping one layer functions – er_plot_show_model() (fitted-model curve/ribbon summary), er_plot_show_quantiles() (exposure-quantile-binned response summary), er_plot_show_data() (strip depicting raw data), /er_plot_show_groups() (grouped exposure-distribution panels) – render plot()/print(), build ggplot2/patchwork objects directly er_plot_build(). er_plot() never fits model ; model implementing small interface er_model_interface can passed er_plot_show_model().

title: The exposure-response plotting mini-language — er_plot
text: data Observed data exposure Exposure variable (one variable, unquoted) response Response variable (one variable, unquoted) stratify_by Stratification variable used color fill (one variable, unquoted); see "Stratification" response_type One "auto" (default), "binary", "continuous", "count". Governs response-scale defaults (e.g. axis limits) summary/CI method quantile VPC layers use. "auto", response column classified "binary" logical takes values {0, 1}, "continuous" otherwise – means count (Poisson-style) response, e.g. adverse-event count, auto-detects "continuous" (counts confined {0, 1}) summarised approximately-continuous quantity (bin mean plus t-interval). "auto" never resolves "count": pass response_type = "count" explicitly genuine count response instead get bin mean plus exact Poisson interval (see poisson_interval()), – unlike t-interval approximation – never produces negative lower bound. See PLAN.md's design decision (4) rationale.

what: Layers are either singleton or additive
title: The exposure-response plotting mini-language — er_plot
text: er_plot_show_model(), er_plot_show_quantiles(), er_plot_show_data() singleton: calling one twice object overwrites first call's result rather combining two. er_plot_show_groups() additive: call adds another grouped-distribution panel alongside already added, rather replacing . asymmetry deliberate, accidental – one "model" one "quantile summary" show per plot, many legitimate ways slice exposure distribution different grouping variables. See PLAN.md's "Mini-language architecture review" design discussion, including one flagged future exception: overlaying two model curves comparison currently supported, one singleton layer additive variant might eventually make sense.

title: The exposure-response plotting mini-language — er_plot
text: stratify_by (set , er_plot()) declares single discrete variable used split layers color/fill, one shared, deduplicated legend across whole composed plot. layer function's keep_strata argument controls whether layer actually uses stratification (defaults TRUE whenever stratify_by set, FALSE otherwise). er_plot_show_data() partial exception "always color/fill" rule continuous/count response: color aesthetic already spoken response value , stratification falls back one panel per stratum level instead shared legend – see documentation PLAN.md general "layer's encoding takes precedence" rule follows.

code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ library(erglm) mod <- erglm_model(ae1 ~ aucss, erglm_data, family = binomial()) erglm_data |>   er_plot(aucss, ae1) |>   er_plot_show_model(mod) |>   er_plot_show_quantiles() |>   plot()  # continuous response: bin means/t-intervals instead of rates/ # Clopper-Pearson intervals, auto-detected from the response column mod3 <- erglm_model(biomarker_change ~ aucss, erglm_data, family = gaussian()) erglm_data |>   er_plot(aucss, biomarker_change) |>   er_plot_show_model(mod3) |>   er_plot_show_quantiles() |>   plot()  # count response: declare response_type = "count" explicitly for an # exact Poisson interval instead of the t-interval approximation used # by the auto-detected ("continuous") default mod4 <- erglm_model(ae_count ~ aucss, erglm_data, family = poisson()) erglm_data |>   er_plot(aucss, ae_count, response_type = "count") |>   er_plot_show_model(mod4) |>   er_plot_show_quantiles() |>   plot() } # }

what: Visual predictive check plot for an exposure-response model — er_vpc_plot
title: Visual predictive check plot for an exposure-response model — er_vpc_plot
text: Compares observed response rates simulated response rates model, stratified grouping variable. function model-agnostic: operates purely data frames. sim data frame expected contain one row per simulated observation per replicate, sim_id column identifying replicate (see e.g. erglm::erglm_vpc_sim() one way generate simulations fitted model).

title: Visual predictive check plot for an exposure-response model — er_vpc_plot
code: er_vpc_plot(   data,   sim,   exposure,   response,   group_by,   conf_level = 0.95,   response_type = c("auto", "binary", "continuous", "count") )

title: Visual predictive check plot for an exposure-response model — er_vpc_plot
text: data Observed data sim Simulated data, exposure/response/group_by columns data, plus sim_id column identifying replicate exposure Exposure variable (one variable, unquoted) response Response variable (one variable, unquoted). May binary (0/1, logical) continuous; see response_type group_by Variable (unquoted) stratify predictions conf_level Confidence level response_type One "auto" (default), "binary", "continuous", "count". Governs observed-side summary computed: response rate Clopper-Pearson CI "binary", bin mean t-interval "continuous" (see t_interval()), bin mean exact Poisson interval "count" (see poisson_interval()). "auto" detects observed response column (entirely {0, 1}, logical, treated binary; see er_plot()'s response_type heuristic) never resolves "count": count (Poisson-style) response auto-detects "continuous" (counts confined {0, 1}) summarised bin-mean-plus-t-interval approximation unless response_type = "count" declared explicitly, case exact Poisson interval used instead – see PLAN.md's design decision (4) rationale.

title: Exact Poisson confidence interval for a count rate — poisson_interval
text: x Vector (sum) observed counts, e.g. counts falling one exposure bin n Number units counts accumulated (e.g. number observations bin); rate estimated sum(x) / n conf_level Confidence level

title: Exact Poisson confidence interval for a count rate — poisson_interval
text: Named numeric vector (lower, upper) rate sum(x) / n, confidence level stored attribute. Uses standard exact ("Garwood") Poisson interval, derived chi-squared/gamma relationship, rather normal approximation. total count 0, lower bound 0 (gamma quantile shape = 0).

title: Exact Poisson confidence interval for a count rate — poisson_interval
text: count-response analogue clopper_pearson(), used quantile-binned summary layer (see er_plot_show_quantiles()) er_vpc_plot() response_type = "count" explicitly declared. Unlike t_interval() (default, opt--required approximation used count response auto-detects declared "continuous"), interval exact never produces negative lower bound – see PLAN.md design decision (4) rationale history.

title: Exact Poisson confidence interval for a count rate — poisson_interval
code: poisson_interval(3, 10) #>      lower      upper  #> 0.06186721 0.87672731  #> attr(,"conf_level") #> [1] 0.95
